p0.problem: CODE keyboard focus invisible; r-input-wrap:focus-within exists at line 178; WCAG 2.4.7
blocking alert, no inline cause, no retry; empty-states + async_reliability
p0.proposed: replace with inline joinErr state, role=alert

p0.problem: inline error needs token style; reuse var(--danger-ink)

p0.current: <button className="r-switch" data-on={String(room.autoAllow)} disabled={!isHost} title={isHost ? "Auto-approve agent edits" : "Only the host can change auto-allow"} onClick={toggleAutoAccept} />
p0.problem: This is the single highest-blast-radius control in the app (it gates whether agent edits auto-apply WITHOUT host review). It is a bare <button> with no role="switch", no aria-checked, and no aria-label — a screen reader announces only "button" plus the title, never the on/off state. Violates the agentic-reliability HONEST_STATUS principle (state must be truthfully exposed) and WCAG 4.1.2 Name/Role/Value. The visual on/off (data-on) has no programmatic equivalent.
p0.proposed: <button className="r-switch" role="switch" aria-checked={room.autoAllow} aria-label="Auto-allow agent edits without host review" data-on={String(room.autoAllow)} disabled={!isHost} title={isHost ? "Auto-approve agent edits" : "Only the host can change auto-allow"} onClick={toggleAutoAccept} />

p0.problem: Busy state is text-only ("Uploading...") with no spinner and no aria-busy/aria-live. The skeleton-vs-spinner convention requires an inline SPINNER + label when the result shape is unpredictable (an upload's parsed output is) — never a silent label swap. It is also an honesty/a11y gap: a screen-reader user gets `disabled` with no announced status, so the upload appears to do nothing (async_reliability: user must always see in-flight state). The static Upload icon should become an animated spinner.

The error state is a bare line of text with no recovery path. The empty-state error convention (empty-states.md rule 3) and async_reliability ("partial success is first-class; user always sees which item failed and why; never silent on a failure path; always expose a manual Retry") require: plain cause + a Retry primary + dismissibility. As shipped, a failed parse leaves the user stuck re-finding the upload button with the error still glued under it. Add a Retry that re-opens the picker and a dismiss, with role="alert".

p0.element: Public-agent person row vs member row (.r-person live dot)
p0.problem: Every human member renders a <span className="r-dot-live" /> presence dot, but the public-agent row — which carries a live {s.status} — has NO dot. The People section header says "N live" and counts only members.length, so an active agent is present in the list yet excluded from the live grammar. This is an honesty/consistency violation: two rows in the same list use different presence vocabularies for the same "is live" fact. Render the dot for running agents (tinted accent to read as agent, not human-green), or render a muted/idle dot when not running so the row's status is never silently dropped.

p0.surface: Chat — public panel header / job telemetry
p0.element: Long-job status tag in .r-panel-head (the <span className="r-tag"> with Timer + longJob.status)
p0.current: {longJob && {longJob.status} {longJob.attempts}/{longJob.maxAttempts}}
p0.problem: HONEST_STATUS / semantic-color-by-state violation. A failed or blocked job renders with the exact same neutral grey .r-tag chrome as a running or completed job — the header gives no danger signal, so a failure is indistinguishable from in-flight work. Convention: 'Finance/status values color by state, not has-content' and async_reliability 'user always sees which item failed and why'; --danger token must pass contrast in both themes (styles.css:26/36). There is no .r-tag danger variant at all (grep confirmed).

p0.current: Enter and Arrows always preventDefault here
p0.problem: keydown eats Enter and Arrows so submit advances tour
p0.proposed: early return when focus is in an input field

p1.current:
{autoAcceptModal && (
  <div className="r-modal-backdrop" role="presentation">
    <div className="r-modal" role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby="auto-accept-title">
      <button className="r-iconbtn r-modal-x" aria-label="Close" onClick={() => setAutoAcceptModal(false)}><X size={15} /></button>
p1.problem: A blocking modal (role=dialog, aria-modal=true, scrim) that does NOT trap focus, has no initial focus, does not close on Esc, and the backdrop (role=presentation) has no click-to-dismiss. Violates the 'Dismissibility + focus trapping' convention (Carbon modal a11y: blocking modals trap focus, Esc always closes, restore focus to trigger; WCAG 2.1.2 No Keyboard Trap / 2.4.3 Focus Order / 3.2.1). Keyboard users can Tab out of the dialog onto the dimmed page behind it.
p1.proposed: Add: a ref to the dialog + useEffect on autoAcceptModal that (a) stores document.activeElement, (b) focuses the first interactive control (Carbon transactional => first input, here the 'Keep review on' ghost or the checkbox), (c) installs a keydown listener: Esc => setAutoAcceptModal(false); Tab => wrap focus within the dialog, and (d) on close restores focus to the toggle trigger. Also make the backdrop dismiss: <div className="r-modal-backdrop" role="presentation" onClick={() => setAutoAcceptModal(false)}> with the inner .r-modal stopping propagation: onClick={(e)=>e.stopPropagation()}.

p1.current:
<button className="r-iconbtn" title="Toggle light / dark" onClick={() => { const n = dark ? "light" : "dark"; document.documentElement.dataset.theme = n; setDark(!dark); }}>
  {dark ? <Moon size={16} /> : <Sun size={16} />}
</button>
p1.problem: Every other top-bar icon button carries an aria-label (panel toggles, tour, leave); this one has only a title attribute, which many screen readers do not announce reliably for icon-only buttons. It also exposes no toggle state. Inconsistent with the established two-cue/labeled-icon-button convention used across the same bar (Looks-clickable-must-act + accessible-name discipline).
p1.proposed:
<button className="r-iconbtn" aria-label={dark ? "Switch to light theme" : "Switch to dark theme"} aria-pressed={dark} title="Toggle light / dark" onClick={() => { const n = dark ? "light" : "dark"; document.documentElement.dataset.theme = n; setDark(!dark); }}>
  {dark ? <Moon size={16} /> : <Sun size={16} />}
</button>

p1.problem: On copy success the icon swaps Copy->Check visually, but there is no accessible announcement — the aria-label stays 'Copy room code XYZ' and nothing is exposed to a live region, so a screen-reader user gets no confirmation the core multiplayer invite action succeeded. Violates the optimistic/honest-feedback convention ('ack the action; success must be perceivable, not silent'). Also: clipboard write can reject (no navigator.clipboard, permission denied) and the .then has no .catch, so a failed copy silently shows nothing — an honest-status gap.
p1.proposed: Reflect state in the accessible name and announce it: aria-label={codeCopied ? `Room code ${room.code} copied` : `Copy room code ${room.code}`} and add an off-screen live region near the button: {codeCopied ? "Room code copied" : ""}. Add error handling: .then(...).catch(() => setCopyError(true)) and surface a brief 'Copy failed' state instead of a no-op.

p1.element: Person rows (.r-person) — inert rows borrowing clickable hover
p1.current:
.r-person { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 9px; padding: 7px 9px; border-radius: 9px; }
.r-person:hover { background: var(--bg-hover); }
p1.problem: Person rows are plain <div>s with no onClick, yet .r-person:hover paints the same var(--bg-hover) fill the clickable .r-file rows use. This is the looks-clickable-must-act violation in reverse: an inert element borrows a button's hover affordance, implying an action that does not exist. The NetSuite inert row was correctly fixed (.r-file-static:hover { background: transparent }, line 136) — apply the same discipline here. Either drop the hover fill, or (if a future click is intended) make the row a real button. Minimal honest fix: remove the hover background.

p1.current: .r-file[data-active="true"] { background: var(--accent-tint); border-color: var(--accent-border); }
p1.problem: The selected row adds a visible border-color (--accent-border) around the whole row. The row-selection grammar is explicit: hover and selected are BOTH background changes, never a border or left-rail accent on the row (shadcn TableRow data-[state=selected]:bg-muted; open-codesign selectedBg #eef2ff is a fill, not a stroke). The selected fill should be a tint of the accent (already correct: --accent-tint + the icon wash at line 141 gives the required second cue), so the border is redundant and breaks the convention. Drop the border and let the tint + icon-chip wash carry selection.

p1.element: Artifact file rows + Upload + NetSuite (icon-only, no accessible name)
p1.problem: The row's only programmatic hint that it is selectable is the title tooltip ("Drag into chat…"), which describes the drag affordance, not the click action, and is invisible to keyboard/AT users. The selected row exposes data-active but no aria-current/aria-pressed, so a screen reader cannot tell which file is open. Icon-only lucide glyphs have no aria-label. Convention (encoded prior: tooltips + aria-label on icon-only; two-cue states must also be announced). Add aria-current for the open file and an aria-label that names the action, not just the drag hint.

When store.listArtifacts(roomId) returns [], the Files section renders only the kicker, the upload button, and the inert NetSuite row — there is no first-use empty state. The empty-state convention (empty-states.md rule 1, craft-polish trio) requires a one-sentence purpose + a single primary CTA that creates the first record; a list that silently collapses to just a header fails the "every empty state answers what-do-I-do-next" rule. Add a one-line first-use hint above the upload button when arts.length === 0.

p1.current:
<div className="r-slash" role="listbox" aria-label="Commands">
  {SLASH_CMDS.map((c) => (
    <button key={c.label} className="r-slash-item" role="option" aria-selected="false" onMouseDown={(e) => { e.preventDefault(); applySlash(c.insert); }}>
p1.problem: Keyboard convention violation. role="listbox"/role="option" promises arrow-key navigation but aria-selected is hardcoded "false" on every option forever, there is no ArrowUp/ArrowDown handler, and the only activator is onMouseDown (mouse-only). A keyboard user opening the menu with '/' can only Tab to insert SLASH_CMDS[0] (onKeyDown line 221) — they cannot reach options 2-4 or see a selection. Violates 'keyboard support on all interactive elements' and the listbox ARIA contract. .r-slash-item also has no :focus/:focus-visible style distinct from :hover (grep confirmed only :hover at styles.css:186).

p1.current:
:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--accent-primary); outline-offset: 2px; }
.r-send { width: 32px; height: 32px; ... background: var(--accent-primary); color: #fff; transition: filter .15s, transform .08s; }
p1.problem: Two-cue/visible-focus convention violation. The global focus ring is the same hue (--accent-primary) as the Send button's fill. A keyboard user tabbing to Send gets an orange ring 2px outside an orange button — effectively invisible (the only separation is the 2px offset gap on the page bg). Convention: 'Focus rings visible on keyboard nav'; the project's own --focus-ring token (a tinted halo, styles.css:29) exists for exactly this but .r-send doesn't use it.

p1.current:
.r-lane-btn { ... color: var(--text-muted); background: transparent; border: 1px solid transparent; transition: background .15s, color .15s; }
.r-lane-btn[data-on="true"] { background: var(--bg-primary); color: var(--text-primary); border-color: var(--line); box-shadow: var(--shadow-sm); }
p1.problem: Missing hover state. Only the active (data-on="true") lane is styled; the inactive Private/Room button has NO :hover rule (grep confirmed). Hovering 'Room' while in Private mode (or vice-versa) gives zero feedback, so the user can't tell it's the clickable target. Violates the craft-polish self-check ('every clickable element does something observable on hover; pure hover that does nothing doesn't count') and two-cue hover. transition already lists background+color, so the property animation is wired but no hover target color is set.
p1.proposed: .r-lane-btn:not([data-on="true"]):hover { background: var(--bg-hover); color: var(--text-secondary); }

p1.surface: Chat — public panel header / job telemetry
p1.current:
<button className="r-iconbtn r-iconbtn-sm" title={jobBusy === "cancel" ? "Cancelling…" : "Cancel long-running job"} aria-label="Cancel long-running job" data-testid="job-cancel" disabled={jobBusy !== null} onClick={cancelJob}>
  <X size={13} />
</button>
p1.problem: Busy state not visible. While the cancel/retry mutation is in flight the button is disabled and the only busy signal is the title attribute ('Cancelling…') — a desktop hover-only tooltip that never appears for keyboard or touch users, and the X / RefreshCw icon stays static. Convention: 'assistant-ui honest in-flight status' / instant-feedback ('ack within 100ms, show busy'). A 500-1000ms cancel currently looks like a dead button. The .r-iconbtn:disabled state just dims to opacity .5 — reads as 'unavailable', not 'working'.

p1.element: failed-send Retry/Dismiss action row (.r-msg-actions with inline opacity:1)
p1.problem: No busy/disabled state on Retry — a second click while retrySend's postMessage promise is in flight fires a duplicate send (no guard, unlike cancelJob/retryJob which gate on jobBusy). Violates async_reliability ('retry without idempotency = double-write') and instant-feedback. Also the Retry icon doesn't reflect the in-flight retry. The failed row itself is honest (red 'failed to send' tag at line 311) — that part is correct, don't change it.

p1.current:
{messages.length === 0 && failedSends.length === 0 &&
No messages yet. Say hello.
}
p1.problem: Empty-state convention violation. First-use empty answers 'what do I do next' with one sentence + ONE primary CTA; here it's a bare faint one-liner with no actionable hint and no differentiation between the public room (where '/ask' is the high-value first action) and the private agent. 'No messages yet. Say hello.' is the 'never ship a screen that only says Nothing here' anti-pattern. The composer is present below, but a first-use room should point to its hero action (the slash command) rather than a generic greeting.

p1.current:
.r-msg-actions { display: flex; gap: 6px; margin-top: 5px; opacity: 0; transition: opacity var(--motion-fast); }
.r-msg:hover .r-msg-actions, .r-msg:focus-within .r-msg-actions { opacity: 1; }
p1.problem: Touch reachability gap. Copy / Edit / Promote are gated entirely behind :hover/:focus-within. On a coarse pointer (no hover, and tapping a non-focusable bubble div doesn't trigger :focus-within) these actions are unreachable — there is no @media (pointer: coarse) fallback for .r-msg-actions (the stylesheet adds coarse fallbacks for .r-iconbtn/.r-switch/.r-postit-delete at styles.css:515-528 but not here). Promote (the private→public hand-off) and Edit become touch-invisible. Violates 'touch floor / coarse pointer' convention and craft-polish 'every clickable element reachable'.

The row is keyboard-focusable (`tabIndex={0}`, Enter/Space toggles it) but has NO focus-visible style. The global `:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--accent-primary) }` lands on a <tr>, which most engines either drop or clip against the `overflow:hidden` <td>s — so a keyboard user tabbing the grid sees nothing. Violates 'Focus rings visible on keyboard nav' and the two-cue rule (craft-polish.md 57-61; linear focus-ring mandate). Hover and selected both use background, so focus must too, applied to the cells.

p1.element: .xl-cell (data cells, Artifact.tsx:666/678 — the live class is `xl-cell`, NOT the dead `.r-excel-cell` block at lines 240-241)
p1.current: .xl-cell { position: relative; border: 1px solid #e2e2e2; padding: 1px 5px; height: 20px; white-space: nowrap; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; background: #fff; cursor: cell; }
p1.problem: `.xl-cell` advertises `cursor: cell` and is click-to-select / double-click-to-edit, but has ZERO hover feedback — the styled `:hover` rule lives on `.r-excel-cell` (lines 240-241) which the component never emits. So hovering any cell in the rendered Excel grid does nothing; the only state cue is the post-click green `.sel` outline. Violates 'every clickable element does something observable; hover-fill carries the affordance' and two-cue hover (craft-polish.md self-check; linear dense-grid hover-fill). This is the intentional light paper, so tint with a paper-appropriate grey, not the dark token.

<tbody>
  {visibleRowIds.map((rid) => { ... })}
</tbody>

Violates the empty-state mandate: 'Never ship a screen that only says No data; every empty state answers what-do-I-do-next' with one first-use sentence + one primary CTA (empty-states.md rules 1,4). The Import-accounts control exists in the bar but the body gives zero first-use guidance; a new room looks broken. Add a first-use row that drives to the existing import flow.

p1.problem: During enrich the only feedback is the button label flipping to 'Researching...'; the pending rows sit completely static with no per-row busy cue, even though `.r-status-running` (a blue running pill) is fully defined in CSS but never rendered. Convention: on refresh of already-loaded data keep the rows but show a refresh indicator — don't leave the surface looking idle (loading-skeleton.md rule 5 / Don't line 46; HONEST_STATUS). p2.current:
.r-iconbtn { display: inline-grid; place-items: center; width: 32px; height: 32px; border-radius: 8px; background: transparent; border: 1px solid transparent; color: var(--text-muted); transition: background .15s, color .15s, border-color .15s; font-size: 14px; }
.r-iconbtn:hover { background: var(--bg-hover); color: var(--text-primary); }
p2.problem: The top-bar icon buttons (toggles, tour, theme, leave) have hover + data-on states but NO :active/pressed feedback, while sibling controls in the same stylesheet do (.r-btn:active { transform: translateY(1px) }, .r-send:active { transform: scale(.94) }). Violates the 'Instant feedback / press scale ~0.97, ack within 100ms' convention and creates an inconsistent press grammar within one bar.
p2.proposed: Add: .r-iconbtn { transition: background .15s, color .15s, border-color .15s, transform .08s; } .r-iconbtn:active { transform: scale(.94); } (transform-only, no layout jank; suppressed under the existing prefers-reduced-motion reset).

p2.current: {store.mode === "convex" && ● live convex}
p2.problem: The live badge hardcodes a hex/rgba pair inline, bypassing the token system, while its sibling .r-demo-badge correctly uses --accent-tint/--accent-ink/--accent-border tokens (line 561). The repo already defines --success (#1F8A5B) and --success-ink (#2E9E6B). Hardcoding #2E9E6B means the badge will not track any future success-token theme tuning and ships an inconsistent (no border) shape vs the demo badge. Violates the density-tokens / semantic-color-token discipline ('semantic colors must pass contrast in BOTH themes' and be referenced via tokens).
p2.proposed: Promote to a class. CSS: .r-tag.live { background: rgba(31,138,91,.16); color: var(--success-ink); border: 1px solid rgba(31,138,91,.28); } TSX: {store.mode === "convex" && ● live convex} — matches the demo badge's bordered, tokenized shape.

p2.current: <button className="r-btn primary" onClick={confirmAutoAccept}><ShieldCheck size={14} /> Turn on auto-accept</button>
p2.problem: The .r-modal-icon already renders a ShieldCheck at the top of the dialog (line 220). Repeating ShieldCheck inside the primary CTA doubles the same glyph in one short dialog, diluting it as a signal. The encoded prior convention reserves the accent as 'the scarcest resource' and prefers one clear primary action; a second shield adds noise without information. Polish only.
p2.proposed: Drop the redundant icon: <button className="r-btn primary" onClick={confirmAutoAccept}>Turn on auto-accept</button> (keeps one filled primary per view; the dialog's hero ShieldCheck carries the meaning).

p2.current: {members.slice(0, 4).map((m) => ({initials(m.name)}))} ◆
p2.problem: Avatars are <span>s showing only 2-letter initials with a presence pulse and no accessible label; the agent chip is a bare ◆ glyph. A screen-reader user gets initials like 'HS' or the diamond character with no name, and there is no title on hover to disambiguate who is present — the presence-pulse 'live' state is purely visual. Minor a11y/affordance gap (presence is informational, not interactive, so P2).
p2.proposed: Add titles + roles: and the agent chip: ◆ (also swaps the hardcoded #d97757 for the accent token).

For a consent dialog about auto-applying agent writes, the consequence text should be programmatically associated. Reinforces finding #2's a11y theme.

p2.current:
.r-roomcode { ... cursor: pointer; }
.r-roomcode:hover { background: var(--bg-hover); border-color: var(--border-strong); }
p2.problem: The room-code chip's only explicit state change is :hover (bg + border). Keyboard focus falls back to the global :focus-visible ring (outline 2px accent) which is correct, but there is no success-state STYLE for the copied state — the chip looks identical when armed vs copied except the swapped icon. Convention asks state changes to use 'at LEAST two cues'; the copied state currently has one (icon swap). Polish.
p2.proposed: Add a copied class that brightens the border to confirm success: .r-roomcode[data-copied="true"] { border-color: rgba(31,138,91,.4); color: var(--success-ink); } and set data-copied={String(codeCopied)} on the button — pairs the icon swap with a color cue (two cues) consistent with the accept-tint grammar used elsewhere.

p2.current:
<label className="r-checkline">
  <input type="checkbox" checked={rememberAutoAccept} onChange={(e) => setRememberAutoAccept(e.currentTarget.checked)} />
  Remember my preference on this device
</label>
p2.problem: The checkbox uses accent-color: var(--accent-primary) (line 108) but relies entirely on the browser-default focus ring; on Chromium the default ring is faint against the dark --bg-primary modal. There is no explicit :focus-visible treatment, so keyboard focus on the consent checkbox can be near-invisible — the one control that records a persisted preference. Convention: 'visible keyboard focus ring mandatory'. Low impact because the label is large, hence P2.
p2.proposed: Add: .r-checkline input:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--accent-primary); outline-offset: 2px; border-radius: 3px; } (the global :focus-visible already targets this, but make it explicit/rounded for the small box so it reads clearly on the dark surface).

p2.element: Panel toggle group / leave button (busy state during collab run)
p2.current: <button className="r-iconbtn" title="Leave room" aria-label="Leave room" onClick={onLeave}><LogOut size={16} /></button>  // and the 3 panel toggles, with runCollab() forcing setShow({left:true,artifact:true,priv:true})
p2.problem: While a collaboration run is in flight (collab.running), runCollab() force-opens all three panels (line 130) and the agent is actively writing cells, but the top-bar toggles and Leave remain fully active with no busy affordance — a user can toggle a panel closed mid-merge or leave the room during an in-flight CAS write with no indication that something is running. No busy/loading state is surfaced anywhere in the top bar. Per the busy-state requirement (every interactive element should reflect in-flight status), the bar should at least signal the run. Lowest-impact because the engine is no-clobber, hence P2.
p2.proposed: Surface the run in the bar: when collab.running, render an inline run indicator near the live/demo badge, e.g. {collab.running && agent running}, reusing the existing .r-typing dots. Optionally guard onLeave behind a confirm while collab.running so a mid-merge exit is intentional.

p2.current:
NetSuite export
source · read-only
p2.problem: The row is correctly de-clickabled (do not change that), but its read-only status lives only in the meta text "source · read-only" — there is no visual lock affordance and no programmatic role. A locked/inert row should signal its state with a glyph, and AT users get a bare div. Add a small Lock glyph and aria so the read-only nature is conveyed by shape, not color/text alone. Note: this row also lacks the min-width:0 wrapper the real rows have (the inner <span> omits style={{minWidth:0}}), so a long source name would reflow the rail instead of ellipsing.

p2.element: Artifact file row (.r-file) focus-visible ring clipping
p2.current:
:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--accent-primary); outline-offset: 2px; }
.r-rail { flex: 1; overflow-y: auto; padding: 10px 8px; }
p2.problem: File rows inherit only the global :focus-visible outline with outline-offset: 2px. Because the rows sit inside .r-rail { overflow-y: auto }, the 2px-offset ring on the first and last visible rows is clipped by the scroll container's overflow edge, so keyboard focus on a scrolled-to row can show a half ring. Convention: focus rings must be fully visible on keyboard nav. Give .r-file a self-contained inset/box-shadow focus ring (the token --focus-ring exists) that does not depend on outside-the-box space.

p2.element: Upload button — dashed border implies a dropzone that isn't wired
p2.current:
.r-file.r-upload { margin-top: 6px; border-style: dashed; }
// JSX: <button className="r-file r-upload" ... onClick={() => inputRef.current?.click()}>
p2.problem: The dashed border is the universal visual language for a drag-and-drop dropzone, but the upload control only handles onClick → picker; there is no onDrop/onDragOver handler anywhere in LeftRail, and the rail's drag wiring (dragArtifactRef) is drag-FROM rows TO chat, not drop-TO-upload. A user who drags a file onto the dashed target gets nothing (the browser navigates away). Either wire onDrop to onUpload, or drop the dashed styling so it reads as a plain button. Lowest-risk: wire the drop to match the affordance the dashed border already promises.
p2.proposed:
<button
  className="r-file r-upload"
  disabled={uploading}
  aria-busy={uploading}
  onClick={() => inputRef.current?.click()}
  onDragOver={(e) => { e.preventDefault(); e.dataTransfer.dropEffect = "copy"; }}
  onDrop={(e) => { e.preventDefault(); void onUpload(e.dataTransfer.files); }}
>
  ...
</button>
/* now the dashed dropzone affordance is honest: drop files onto it to upload */

p2.element: edit textarea Save button + edit save flow
p2.problem: The optimistic edit is honest (it reverts + shows editErr on failure via role=alert at line 442 — good). But on failure the user is dropped back to read mode with the OLD text and an error line; their typed edit is lost (draft is gone because editing closed). Minor data-loss on the error path. Convention: error states should let the user recover, not silently discard their input. Low impact since edits are short, hence P2.

p2.surface: Chat — composer hint chips & message action buttons
p2.current:
.r-msg-act:hover { background: var(--bg-hover); color: var(--text-primary); }
.r-chip:hover { border-color: var(--accent-border); color: var(--accent-ink); }
p2.problem: No :active press feedback. .r-send has a press transform (scale .94, styles.css:196) and .r-btn has translateY(1px), but the smaller .r-chip and .r-msg-act buttons give no tactile press cue. Convention: 'press scale 0.97 / instant feedback on click'; clicks under 100ms should acknowledge. Pure polish, hence P2 — hover cues are present and correct.
p2.proposed:
.r-msg-act:active, .r-chip:active { transform: translateY(1px); }
.r-msg-act, .r-chip { transition: background var(--motion-fast), color var(--motion-fast), border-color var(--motion-fast), transform .08s; }

p2.element: Bubble pending dim (inline style opacity:0.6 on data-state="pending")
p2.current: <div className={"r-msg" + (agent ? " agent" : "")} data-testid="chat-message" data-clientmsgid={m.clientMsgId} data-state={pending ? "pending" : "confirmed"} style={pending ? { opacity: 0.6 } : undefined}>
p2.problem: The pending dim is an inline style with no matching CSS hook on [data-state="pending"], so it can't be themed/overridden and duplicates intent already carried by the 'sending…' tag (line 414). Discord/Slack optimistic convention wants the pending row faded AND a glyph — the glyph (tag) is there, but driving the fade from CSS keeps the state grammar in one place and lets reduced-motion/theming touch it. Minor consistency issue, P2.

p2.surface: Chat — private composer lane toggle (focus order)
p2.current:
<div className="r-lane" role="group" aria-label="Where your agent acts">
  <button type="button" className="r-lane-btn" data-on={String(!roomLane)} data-testid="lane-private" onClick={() => setRoomLane(false)} ...>
  <button type="button" className="r-lane-btn" data-on={String(roomLane)} data-testid="lane-room" onClick={() => setRoomLane(true)} ...>
p2.problem: This is a single-choice toggle (exactly one lane active) but it's a plain role="group" with two buttons and the active state is conveyed only visually via data-on (background tint). A screen-reader user gets no 'pressed/selected' state. Convention: 'selected/active indicator uses shape or weight, not color alone' applies to AT too — the active lane needs aria-pressed (or radiogroup semantics). Visual state is fine; the a11y semantic is missing. P2.

p2.element: .r-research-detail-row td (expanded detail panel bottom edge, styles.css:418)
p2.current: .r-research-detail-row td { padding: 0; white-space: normal; background: var(--bg-secondary); border-bottom: 2px solid var(--accent-border); }
p2.problem: The expanded detail row closes with a heavy 2px accent rule. Row dividers must be a single 1px near-invisible hairline, never a heavy rule or accent bar (data-table.jsx 290 borderBottom 1px; 'never heavy rules'). It also spends the scarcest resource (accent) on a structural divider, against 'accent is the scarcest resource'. The selected fill (`accent-tint` on the open row's cells) already signals the boundary; the closing edge should be the same hairline as every other row.
p2.proposed: .r-research-detail-row td { padding: 0; white-space: normal; background: var(--bg-secondary); border-bottom: 1px solid var(--line); }

p2.current:
.r-srcchip { display: inline-block; max-width: 100%; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; vertical-align: bottom; color: var(--accent-ink); font-size: 11px; text-decoration: none; }
.r-srcchip + .r-srcchip { margin-left: 6px; max-width: 60px; }
p2.problem: The source chips are bare 11px text links with no padding and a 60px-clamped second chip — well under the 44px coarse-pointer tap floor, and they sit inside a row whose <td> stops propagation, so they're the only thing clickable there. No hover/focus affordance beyond color. Violates 'touch floor 44px on coarse pointers' and 'clickable chips must be real chips with hover'. Give them chip padding + a hover tint + a coarse-pointer min height (they're <a>, so the global focus-visible already covers keyboard).

p2.current:
.r-cell-edit { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 4px; background: transparent; border: none; padding: 2px 4px; margin: -2px -4px; border-radius: 6px; color: inherit; font: inherit; cursor: text; transition: background .12s; }
.r-cell-edit:hover { background: var(--bg-hover); }
p2.problem: The rest-state cell editor is a real <button>, so the global `:focus-visible` outline fires — but the button carries `margin: -2px -4px`, so a 2px outset accent outline is drawn into the neighbouring cell and the cell border, reading as a clipped/offset ring. Inline-edit affordance convention wants a clean keyboard focus on the cell, not a bleeding outline (linear 'focus ring, no glow'; craft-polish two-cue). Replace the outset outline with an inset ring matched to the button's own radius.

p2.current: <button className="r-btn ghost" onClick={() => downloadResearchCsv(art, rowIds, cell)}><Download size={13} /> CRM CSV</button>
p2.problem: The export button is always enabled; with zero accounts it downloads a header-only CSV — an action that looks like it produced data but didn't (a soft HONEST_STATUS gap: the control implies content it can't deliver). Other toolbar buttons already gate on state ('Requeue complete' disables at `complete === 0`). Gate export on having rows so a dead export can't masquerade as a real one.
p2.proposed: <button className="r-btn ghost" disabled={rowIds.length === 0} onClick={() => downloadResearchCsv(art, rowIds, cell)}><Download size={13} /> CRM CSV</button>

p2.current:
.r-postit { position: absolute; width: 168px; ... transition: box-shadow .18s, transform .18s; }
.r-postit:hover { box-shadow: 0 14px 30px -10px rgba(0,0,0,.5); transform: rotate(0deg) scale(1.02); z-index: 5; }
p2.problem: Hover scales the card (`scale(1.02)`) and straightens its rotation. The grid self-check is explicit: 'no row/card/button shifts size on hover or state change' — a scale on hover is exactly the banned size-shift, and on a drag surface it also nudges the apparent drop target. Keep the lift (shadow + small translate) but drop the scale so the footprint is stable; the contentEditable text and delete hit-area no longer jump under the pointer.
p2.proposed: .r-postit:hover { box-shadow: 0 14px 30px -10px rgba(0,0,0,.5); transform: rotate(0deg) translateY(-2px); z-index: 5; }

p2.element: Non-host viewer branch of InlineProposal (Artifact.tsx:829) and ProposalRow (Artifact.tsx:1062)
p2.problem: For a non-host viewer the inline proposal collapses to a bare lowercase 'host' chip (`.r-inline-awaiting`, no tooltip/aria) sitting next to the proposed value. It reads as a stray label, not a state — the user can't tell it means 'a host must approve this'. The verbose ProposalRow already says 'awaiting host review'; the inline pill should carry the same meaning via title+aria so an icon-only/abbreviated control is self-explanatory (icon-only controls need tooltip + aria-label).
p2.proposed: ) : host}

p2.current: .r-research thead th { position: sticky; top: 0; z-index: 3; text-align: left; font-size: 10px; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: .12em; color: var(--text-tertiary); padding: 7px 10px; border-bottom: 1px solid var(--line); font-weight: 600; background: var(--bg-secondary); }
p2.problem: Header label is 10px — below the 11px quiet-label floor (data-table.jsx 261-276 / shadcn TableHead use 11px; this repo's own .r-sheet th and .r-status sit at 10.5px). At 10px uppercase with .12em tracking on `--text-tertiary` (the faintest text token), the column headers are the hardest text in the grid to read. Bump to 11px and one tone up to `--text-muted` so the header reads as a quiet label, not vanishing micro-type. Keep weight/case/tracking.
p2.proposed: .r-research thead th { position: sticky; top: 0; z-index: 3; text-align: left; font-size: 11px; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: .1em; color: var(--text-muted); padding: 7px 10px; border-bottom: 1px solid var(--line); font-weight: 600; background: var(--bg-secondary); }

p2.element: Excel cell double-click-to-edit (Artifact.tsx:687 onDoubleClick) vs locked cells
p2.current:
onClick={() => setSel(elementId)}
onDoubleClick={() => { if (!lk) setEditingId(elementId); }}
p2.problem: Editing is gated entirely behind a double-click and a silent `if (!lk)` — a locked cell already shows the holder outline (.xl-cell.locked) and `cursor: not-allowed`, which is good, but on an UNlocked cell there is no signal that double-click edits, and on a locked cell the double-click is a silent no-op with no explanation (the same lock that surfaces a name flag could surface 'locked by X' on the attempt). This is a discoverability/honest-feedback polish gap. Minimal fix: on a double-click of a locked cell, route the existing lock reason through onError so the attempt isn't silently swallowed.
p2.proposed:
onClick={() => setSel(elementId)}
onDoubleClick={() => { if (lk) { onError({ ok: false, reason: "locked" } as EditFeedback); } else { setEditingId(elementId); } }}

p2.current: background scrim at 62 percent opacity
p2.problem: exceeds the 0.40 to 0.55 spotlight ceiling
p2.proposed: lower the scrim to 50 percent

p2.current: radius fixed at 12px in CSS
p2.problem: ring ignores the target corner radius
p2.proposed: pass target radius into a CSS var

p2.current: always enabled with no busy state
p2.problem: double click can skip a step before target appears
p2.proposed: disable until the target rect resolves

p2.current: changes only background to the accent
p2.problem: active versus inactive differs by color alone
p2.proposed: add a width or shape cue to the active dot

p2.current: aria live on the outer container
p2.problem: double announces with the dialog aria label
p2.proposed: move aria live to the title heading

p2.current: skip rendered on every step
p2.problem: on the last step skip duplicates done
p2.proposed: render skip only when not the last step

p2.current: reduced read once with no listener
p2.problem: mid session toggle does not flip the flag
p2.proposed: add a matchMedia change listener
